Mount Helicon · open source · local-first

Your agent trusts everything it remembers.

Yesterday, 30% of what my agent retrieved was rot: killed notes, dead project names, a birthday two files disagree on. Mount Helicon is the test layer that catches it: ten documented failure classes, checked live against your real memory, every finding with its receipt, on top of any store. Never a store itself.

Run the exam on your stack GitHub Live dashboard

The research is unambiguous

<16%of the reasoning behind conflicting sources is recovered by GPT-4.1-class models. Humans double every score. CONFRAG, ACL 2026
64%of memory-agent errors trace to outdated memory that was never forgotten. Measured on GPT-5.2, Gemini 3, Qwen3. Memora, 2026
68→28%accuracy collapse on superseded facts as history grows, measured up to gpt-5.4. More memory recovers nothing. Supersede, 2026
6.3%of contradictions get flagged unprompted; ~88% when explicitly asked. Nothing in production asks. A check layer is the asking. WikiContradict
"Memory quality failures are silent... an agent giving bad advice due to stale memories looks just like one giving good advice."
A production user requesting staleness detection, conflict detection and retrieval-quality metrics from their memory vendor. Closed "not planned" the next day, June 2026. That request is this tool's feature list.

What it looks like in practice

MY OWN STORE

4 of 13 retrieval tasks were serving rot. The battery names the exact memories and why each is wrong. A contradiction about a real birthday surfaced unprompted, with both conflicting lines side by side, and was resolved with one command.

A STORE I DON'T OWN

Pointed the exam at openai/codex's AGENTS.md across 27 real commits: 5 rule sections drifted, retrieval regressed. One command, cited by SHA, reproducible by anyone.

VS HUMAN LABELS

Five agents manually audited my second brain and stamped 21 dated rot banners. The deterministic exam, answer key stripped: 6/16 file-level catches, zero LLM. It also found a win-count fight between my resume and my notes that the humans missed. Misses are published, not hidden.

1EXAMten failure classes run against your live store, on a timer
2RECEIPTevery finding shows its evidence: the two lines that cannot both be true
3RULINGyou decide once: keep, kill, or resolve with the truth
4NEVER TWICEresolved rot re-alarms if it ever comes back

Setup: four commands, read-only

# any machine with python 3.11+
git clone https://github.com/MorkeethHQ/mount-helicon && cd mount-helicon
pip install -e .

helicon init     # detects Claude Code, Cursor, Obsidian, your git repos
helicon scan     # read-only ingest into Helicon's own store
helicon rot      # the exam: 10 classes, deterministic, zero LLM, free

# optional, the ambient mode:
helicon watch --install   # cron every 6h; notifies ONLY on new drift

A Qwen key (Alibaba Model Studio free tier) unlocks the judged tests: live contradiction and grounding verdicts, natural-language triage rules. Keyless, every deterministic check still runs and says so. Your sources are read, never written; corrections live in Helicon's store, not your files; write-backs are dry-run with backups, always yours to apply. The full access model is in the README.

Mount Helicon

CI for agent memory.
We don't store it. We fail the build when it rots. You rule, it seals in gold.

helicon rot, on your stack
